title: Spicy Mexican Chili Quesadilla
description: A pungent, chewy, one-pot mexican dish featuring a mother sauce base and double cream, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This egg-free-friendly recipe is a twist on the classic chili quesadilla.

ingredients.name: Shredded cheese
ingredients.notes: Monterey Jack or Cheddar work well

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the ancho chilies, poblanos, cumin, coriander, and gochujang to the pot, stirring to combine.
Cook for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the spices are fragrant.
Add the diced onion mixture to the pot, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 10 minutes.
Stir in the double cream and cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until heated through.
Tear the shredded cheese into 4 equal pieces and place one piece in the center of each tortilla.
Place a spoonful of the chili mixture on top of the cheese, then fold the tortilla in half to enclose.
Cook the quesadillas in a large skillet over medium heat for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until the cheese is melted and the tortillas are crispy.
Cut the quesadillas into wedges and serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.
Garnish with additional cilantro, if desired.
